steps.title: Distribute the negative sign when subtracting b
steps.content:
To find $a - b$, distribute the negative sign across all terms in $b$:

$$a - b = (6k + 5r) - (9k - 12r + 3)$$
$$= 6k + 5r - 9k + 12r - 3$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms by variable:
  • $k$ terms: $6k - 9k = -3k$
  • $r$ terms: $5r + 12r = 17r$
  • Constants: $-3$

This gives us $a - b = -3k + 17r - 3$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
